summary: Live confirmation (Pro account) of 3 wireframe deviations speculated in MV-analytics-002: Export button absent, header freshness label absent, Interview-Conversion line chart replaced by a Stage-Conversion percentage grid.
reproduction: 1. Login as admin/admin123 (Pro entitlement) at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login
2. Navigate to /dashboard/analytics
3. Search the full-page DOM/screenshot for any element containing the text 'Export' — none found (wireframe data-design-id=btn-export-an05 has no implementation)

4. Inspect the header subtitle under the 'Analytics' H1 — it reads 'Funnel conversion, ATS score quality and agent spend.' with no '· updated N min ago' freshness timestamp anywhere on the page (wireframe topbar-an03)

5. Locate the second grid card (wireframe data-design-id=conversion-an08, 'Interview Conversion' SVG line chart with W1-W4 x-axis) — the real UI instead renders a 'Stage Conversion (all)' section with 4 percentage tiles (Found→Applied, Applied→Screened, Screened→Interview, Interview→Offer), each backed by live GET /analytics/conversion data
expected: Per wireframe: an Export button in the header, a freshness label ('updated N min ago') next to the range label, and an SVG line chart titled 'Interview Conversion' showing week-over-week trend.
observed: No Export button exists anywhere in the rendered DOM. No freshness/updated-N-min-ago text exists anywhere on the page. The Interview-Conversion line chart is not rendered; a 4-tile 'Stage Conversion' percentage grid (data-testid=conversion-rates) is shown instead, driven by real GET /analytics/conversion data (values verified to match direct SQL recomputation, see MV note in report). Functionally the substitution surfaces real conversion-rate data (the wireframe's line chart used static/decorative example points with no real weekly time-series backing it), so this is arguably a legitimate design substitution rather than a broken feature, but it is a visible deviation from the wireframe per protocol §3.2.1.

summary: The 7d/30d/90d/All period selector only re-fetches and re-renders the Application Funnel and Stage Conversion panels; the Dashboard-summary metric cards, ATS Score Distribution, and Agent ROI panels never receive a period parameter and never change when the period is switched, despite sitting on the same page under the same selector with no visual cue that they are exempt.
reproduction: 1. Login as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/analytics with browser network capture on
2. Record the rendered text of the Dashboard-summary cards, ATS Score Distribution panel, and Agent ROI panel while period='all' is selected
3. Click the '7d' period pill

4. Observe the network requests fired: GET /analytics/funnel?period=7d and GET /analytics/conversion?period=7d fire with the new period; GET /analytics/dashboard, GET /analytics/ats-distribution, and GET /analytics/agent-roi either do not fire a period-scoped variant or (for /analytics/dashboard) fire with NO period query param at all
5. Re-read the Dashboard-summary/ATS/Agent-ROI panel text after the click — byte-identical to before the click
expected: Either all analytics panels on the page respect the selected period, or panels that are permanently all-time carry a visible label/disclaimer distinguishing them from the period-scoped panels, so a user is not misled into thinking the whole page just re-scoped to '7d'.
observed: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx calls fetchDashboard() with no period argument (apps/web/src/lib/api/analytics.ts:80-82 — fetchDashboard(options) never accepts/forwards a period), and fetchAtsDistribution()/fetchAgentRoi() call endpoints that have no period parameter at all server-side (apps/api/app/routers/analytics.py ats_distribution() and agent_roi() take no period argument). Confirmed live: after clicking '7d', the network log shows GET /analytics/dashboard (no query string) and GET /analytics/ats-distribution / GET /analytics/agent-roi (no period support), while GET /analytics/funnel?period=7d and GET /analytics/conversion?period=7d correctly re-fire. Dashboard-summary/ATS/Agent-ROI panel text was confirmed unchanged before/after the period click in two independent sessions.
evidence: apps/api/app/routers/analytics.py:67-114 (ats_distribution/agent_roi have no period param, source citation)

summary: Dashboard-summary 'Applications' metric card (14) and the Application-Funnel 'Applied' stage (7) show materially different numbers for what a user would reasonably read as the same metric, on the same screen, same account, same period, with no UI cue explaining the discrepancy.
reproduction: 1. Login as admin/admin123 (Pro), navigate to /dashboard/analytics
2. Read the top-left Dashboard-summary card: label 'Applications', value 14, tooltip 'Total roles you've applied to, across all sources and time periods.'
3. Scroll to the 'Application Funnel (all)' section directly below and read the 'Applied' bar: value 7

4. Both are on-screen simultaneously; no annotation clarifies that 'Applications'=14 counts ALL Application rows (including drafts never submitted) while funnel 'Applied'=7 counts only non-draft (submitted) applications
expected: Either both figures use the same definition of 'applications', or the UI clearly differentiates them (e.g. 'Applications (incl. drafts)' vs 'Submitted'), so a paying user auditing their funnel does not see two different totals for what reads as one concept.
observed: Direct SQL recomputation against the production DB for this user confirms the root cause: 14 total Application rows break down as 7 draft + 6 submitted + 1 rejected. Dashboard-summary counts all 14 (apps/api/app/routers/analytics.py _dashboard(): 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM "Application" WHERE "userId"=%s'). Funnel 'applied' counts only the 7 non-draft rows (funnel(): 'COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE "status" <> \'draft\')'). Both numbers are individually real and non-fabricated (0.0% delta vs direct SQL), but the screen presents them side-by-side without disambiguation, which is confusing/misleading for a paying user cross-checking their funnel math.
evidence: test-artifacts/paid/26-tooltip-open-proper.png (shows both '14' Applications card and '7' Applied funnel bar in the same screenshot)
test-artifacts/paid/db-groundtruth-sql-recompute.txt (direct SQL: total_applications=14, funnel applied=7, status breakdown draft=7/submitted=6/rejected=1)

summary: 'Top Skills in Demand' panel renders zero content and no empty-state message when the account has no skill-lexicon matches, unlike a loading skeleton or an honest 'no data yet' message.
reproduction: 1. Login as admin/admin123 (Pro), navigate to /dashboard/analytics
2. Scroll to the 'Top Skills in Demand' card (data-testid=top-skills) inside the Real-Time Market Pulse section
3. Observe: heading renders, but the body area below it is entirely blank (no bars, no text, no 'no data' message)
4. Cross-check GET /analytics/market-pulse response: topSkills: [] (empty array — this account's Job.requirements text did not match any term in the backend's curated _SKILL_LEXICON)
expected: An honest empty-state message (e.g. 'No skill data yet — matched jobs will populate this once your Scout agent finds postings with recognized skill keywords') consistent with the honest-empty-state pattern already used elsewhere on this screen (e.g. Market vs. You explicitly states 'Market data: not connected').
observed: apps/web/src/components/analytics/MarketPulse.tsx renders `{data.topSkills.map(...)}` with no fallback branch for an empty array, so the card silently shows nothing. Not fabrication (the empty array itself is real/correct), but fails the honest-empty-state bar applied elsewhere on the same screen.

summary: Wireframe's 'Applications by Source' donut (application channel: LinkedIn/Seek/Indeed) is implemented as 'Jobs by Source' (job-discovery/ATS platform: Greenhouse/Ashby/Lever/RemoteOK/Remotive) — a different underlying metric than the wireframe specifies, though honestly relabeled and not misleading in isolation.
reproduction: 1. Login as admin/admin123 (Pro), navigate to /dashboard/analytics

2. Compare wireframe design/screens/analytics.html data-design-id=sources-an09 ('Applications by Source', legend LinkedIn 49% / Seek 30% / Indeed 21%, center label 'applied') against the live 'Jobs by Source' card (data-testid=sources-donut)
3. Live card shows Greenhouse 36% / Ashby 34% / Lever 21% / Remoteok 7% / Remotive 2%, center label '44 jobs sourced'
expected: Per wireframe: a donut of which application channel (job board) each application was submitted through.
observed: The live donut is sourced from Job.source (ATS/scraper platform the Scout agent discovered the listing from), not Application.source/channel — a genuinely different data dimension. The backend code explicitly documents this as an intentional, honestly-labeled substitution (apps/api/app/routers/analytics.py comment: sourcesLabel 'jobs sourced' ... not applications — the caption must say so honestly, GAP-P4-058), and the values trace exactly to real DB rows (verified via direct API cross-check). Filed for wireframe-conformance completeness per §3.2.1; not a fabrication or dishonesty issue since the live label ('Jobs by Source' / 'jobs sourced') does not claim to be application-channel data.
